threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot a newcomer by their confusion at the bus routes – they still look at a map instead of knowing to just hop on any 149.
Just spotted a guy in a crocodile onesie on the 59 last night—he started singing old soul tracks. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a kid riding their bike on the sidewalk while their grandma shouted at a pigeon. That's Berlin in a nutshell.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you catch a spontaneous outdoor rave under an overpass after dark. The bass reverberates, and it feels like a secret just for us.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local if they can name at least three taco trucks with their salsas and still have a favorite burrito spot that’s not Taqueria Cancun.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus full of parents and toddlers singing 'Baby Shark' at the top of their lungs. Only in the Mission, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often forget that the local bakeries close by 8 PM—no fresh bread after dinner, just stale regrets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them at the Sagrada Família staring up with wide eyes, still fumbling with their metro maps and wondering why the L2 is down.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot a newbie by their confused look when the 106 bus goes off-route — it’s like a local rite of passage figuring it out.
Caught the N68 last night, two blokes debating if a kebab's worth the midnight trek. One swore it tasted better at 3 AM than any posh restaurant.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: One evening, I saw a group of kids playing soccer in the street while their grandmas chatted on the stoops. That mix of generations is classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you watch a DJ spin records at a laundromat while you wait for your clothes. Just another Wednesday night vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're legit when they know not to bother with the 14 at rush hour and have a secret Muni hack for the hidden routes.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a kid on the 24th St. bus doing a magic trick with his phone. He had the whole ride laughing and forgot we were stuck in traffic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss how quiet Gràcia's plazas get after 10 PM. It's like a different world, where locals actually unwind away from the bustle.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll spot them in Gràcia, clutching a map and looking up at the street signs like they’re in a scavenger hunt. It's cute but a bit sad.
